title: Skandamata Chalisa
intro: Shri Skandamata Chalisa: Full Path, Meaning, and Benefits - Hymn to Goddess Skandamata
description:
|| Shri Skandamata Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Simhasangata nityam padmashritkar-dvaya.
Shubhadastu sada devi Skandamata Yashaswini.

|| Chaupai ||
Jayati jayati jagat janani, jai Skanda mata.
Deenan par karuna karati, sada sukhdata.
Simha vahini Durga, dhari Skanda roop.
Putra Kartikeya ke karan, jag mein prasiddh.
Pahadon par vaas kare, simh sang savari.
Kamalasan par virajati, shubhra moorat pyari.
Atyant manohar roop hai, mamta ki moorat.
Kripa drishti se bhare sada, bhaktan ke sankat.
Chaturbhuja dhari mata, shwet kamal pe baithi.
Santan sukh dene wali, dukh haran karti.
Kamal pushp hathon mein, dhare shanti swarupa.
Simha vahan par aarudha, mahima anupam roopa.
Jagat palika hai tu, agh vinashini mata.
Tere charnon mein naman, jayati jai mata.
Dhyan dhare jo bhakt tere, sab kasht mit jaate.
Sakal manorath purn kare, manvanchit phal paate.
Tere dar pe jo bhi aaye, khali na jaata.
Deen dukhi aur nirdhan ka, sankat har jaata.
Sharan mein aaye jo bhi tihari, unko sukh dena.
Bhav sagar se taar kar, bhav bhay har lena.
Kripa drishti sada rakhna, sankat harini mata.
Sakal jagat ki palanhari, jayati jai mata.
Jayati jayati jai Skandamata, hum sabki amba.
Santan sukh dene wali, hamari tum Shambhavi.
Simhasan par virajati, shwet kamal pe baithi.
Deenan par kripa kar, hamare kasht harti.

|| Doha ||
Simhasangata nityam padmashritkar-dvaya.
Shubhadastu sada devi Skandamata Yashaswini.
meaning:
|| Shri Skandamata Chalisa (with English Meaning) ||

|| Doha ||
Simhasangata nityam padmashritkar-dvaya.
(Who sits on a throne and holds lotuses in both hands.)
Shubhadastu sada devi Skandamata Yashaswini.
(May the glorious Goddess Skandamata always grant us auspiciousness.)

|| Chaupai ||
Jayati jayati jagat janani, jai Skanda mata.
(Victory to the Mother of the world, victory to Skandamata.)
Deenan par karuna karati, sada sukhdata.
(You show compassion to the poor and are always the giver of happiness.)
Simha vahini Durga, dhari Skanda roop.
(You are the lion-rider Durga, who took the form of Skanda's mother.)
Putra Kartikeya ke karan, jag mein prasiddh.
(You are famous in the world for being the mother of Kartikeya.)
Pahadon par vaas kare, simh sang savari.
(You dwell on the mountains and ride the lion.)
Kamalasan par virajati, shubhra moorat pyari.
(You sit on a lotus throne, and your radiant form is lovely.)
Atyant manohar roop hai, mamta ki moorat.
(Your form is very charming; you are the image of maternal love.)
Kripa drishti se bhare sada, bhaktan ke sankat.
(Your compassionate glance always removes the troubles of devotees.)
Chaturbhuja dhari mata, shwet kamal pe baithi.
(You are the four-armed Mother, sitting on a white lotus.)
Santan sukh dene wali, dukh haran karti.
(You are the giver of the joy of children and the remover of sorrows.)
Kamal pushp hathon mein, dhare shanti swarupa.
(Holding lotus flowers in your hands, you are the embodiment of peace.)
Simha vahan par aarudha, mahima anupam roopa.
(Mounted on a lion, your glory and form are unique.)
Jagat palika hai tu, agh vinashini mata.
(You are the sustainer of the world, the destroyer of sins.)
Tere charnon mein naman, jayati jai mata.
(Salutations at your feet, victory to you, Mother.)
Dhyan dhare jo bhakt tere, sab kasht mit jaate.
(Devotees who meditate on you find all their miseries erased.)
Sakal manorath purn kare, manvanchit phal paate.
(You fulfill all their desires, and they receive the fruits they seek.)
Tere dar pe jo bhi aaye, khali na jaata.
(Whoever comes to your door never goes empty-handed.)
Deen dukhi aur nirdhan ka, sankat har jaata.
(You take away the troubles of the poor, sad, and needy.)
Sharan mein aaye jo bhi tihari, unko sukh dena.
(Give happiness to those who seek your shelter.)
Bhav sagar se taar kar, bhav bhay har lena.
(Take them across the ocean of existence and remove the fear of life.)
Kripa drishti sada rakhna, sankat harini mata.
(Always keep your compassionate gaze on us, O remover of troubles.)
Sakal jagat ki palanhari, jayati jai mata.
(You are the nurturer of the whole world, victory to you, Mother.)
Jayati jayati jai Skandamata, hum sabki amba.
(Victory to Skandamata, you are our mother.)
Santan sukh dene wali, hamari tum Shambhavi.
(You are the giver of the joy of children, you are our Shambhavi.)
Simhasan par virajati, shwet kamal pe baithi.
(Seated on a throne and a white lotus, show mercy to the needy.)
Deenan par kripa kar, hamare kasht harti.
(Please show mercy to the needy and take away our troubles.)

|| Doha ||
Simhasangata nityam padmashritkar-dvaya.
(Who sits on a throne and holds lotuses in both hands.)
Shubhadastu sada devi Skandamata Yashaswini.
(May the glorious Goddess Skandamata always grant us auspiciousness.)

faq.a1: She is known as 'Skandamata' because she is the mother of Lord Kartikeya (also known as Skanda). She is depicted with the infant Kartikeya in her lap.
faq.q2: What is the main benefit of worshiping her?
faq.a2: Worshiping Maa Skandamata grants salvation (Moksha) to the devotee. Additionally, it is considered highly auspicious for those seeking to be blessed with children or wishing for their children's well-being.
faq.a3: Maa Skandamata governs the 'Vishuddha Chakra' (Throat Chakra). Her worship helps in improving the clarity and sweetness of one's speech.
faq.q4: When is the auspicious day to worship her?
faq.a4: The fifth day of Navratri (Panchami) is dedicated to Maa Skandamata.
faq.q5: What is the appropriate offering (Bhog) for her?
faq.a5: Bananas are considered her favorite offering. It is believed that offering bananas pleases the Goddess, and she blesses her devotees with happiness and prosperity.
